## George H.W. Bush Carrier Strike Group Conducts COMPTUEX Training at Sea

### Overview
George H.W. Bush Carrier Strike Group trains at sea as integrated warfighting team. COMPTUEX prepares naval task forces for high-end Joint combat. Exercise integrates forces for sustained operations.

### Event Details
George H.W. Bush Carrier Strike Group deploys at sea and trains as integrated warfighting team during Composite Training Unit Exercise (COMPTUEX). Navy personnel execute complex scenarios that simulate high-end combat environments. Strike group components coordinate air, surface, and subsurface operations to enhance readiness. COMPTUEX represents Joint Force's most demanding integrated training event. Forces practice sustained combat tactics against peer adversaries. U.S. Navy photo by Mass Communication Specialist 2nd Class Mitchell Mason captures training activities. Strike group hones skills for global deployments, including potential NATO Atlantic missions. Units launch aircraft, conduct missile defense drills, and integrate with allied partners. Exercises emphasize rapid response and multi-domain operations. Commanders assess performance to certify deployment readiness. Training occurs off U.S. East Coast near Norfolk, homeport of US Fleet Forces. Group demonstrates interoperability with NATO allies through shared tactics. Personnel maintain peak proficiency in electronic warfare and strike coordination. COMPTUEX builds capacity for carrier strike group to project power across Atlantic theater. Recent activities align with US Fleet Forces priorities for Atlantic deterrence. Strike group continues preparations for certified combat employment in contested regions.

### Source Context
@USFleetForces (US Fleet Forces) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on us fleet forces norfolk carrier strike group comptuex deployment nato atlantic.
